import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    n = len(cities)
    budget = 0.04 * n

    # Precompute integer distances (TSPLIB EUC_2D)
    xs = [c[0] for c in cities]
    ys = [c[1] for c in cities]

    def dist(i, j):
        dx = xs[i] - xs[j]
        dy = ys[i] - ys[j]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # k-nearest neighbors
    K = min(15, n - 1)
    neighbors = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = [(dist(i, j), j) for j in range(n) if j != i]
        dists.sort()
        neighbors.append([j for _, j in dists[:K]])

    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(dist(tour[i], tour[(i+1) % n]) for i in range(n))

    # Nearest-neighbor construction
    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False] * n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            cur = tour[-1]
            best_d, best_j = float('inf'), -1
            for j in neighbors[cur]:
                if not visited[j]:
                    d = dist(cur, j)
                    if d < best_d:
                        best_d, best_j = d, j
                        break
            if best_j == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        d = dist(cur, j)
                        if d < best_d:
                            best_d, best_j = d, j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = True
        return tour

    # 2-opt with candidate lists and don't-look bits
    def two_opt(tour):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        improved = True
        dont_look = [False] * n
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    return tour
                if dont_look[tour[idx]]:
                    continue
                i = idx
                ci = tour[i]
                i1 = (i + 1) % n
                ci1 = tour[i1]
                d_ci = dist(ci, ci1)
                found = False
                for cj in neighbors[ci]:
                    j = pos[cj]
                    if dist(ci, cj) >= d_ci:
                        break
                    j1 = (j + 1) % n
                    cj1 = tour[j1]
                    if cj == ci or cj1 == ci:
                        continue
                    gain = d_ci + dist(cj, cj1) - dist(ci, cj) - dist(ci1, cj1)
                    if gain > 0:
                        # reverse segment between i1 and j
                        if i1 <= j:
                            tour[i1:j+1] = tour[i1:j+1][::-1]
                        else:
                            tour[j1:i+1] = tour[j1:i+1][::-1]
                        for k2, c2 in enumerate(tour):
                            pos[c2] = k2
                        improved = True
                        found = True
                        dont_look[ci] = False
                        break
                if not found:
                    dont_look[ci] = True
        return tour

    # Or-opt: relocate segments of length 1, 2, 3
    def or_opt(tour):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for seg_len in [1, 2, 3]:
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    return tour
                for i in range(n):
                    if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                        return tour
                    # segment: tour[i], tour[i+1], ..., tour[i+seg_len-1]
                    prev_i = (i - 1) % n
                    end_i = (i + seg_len - 1) % n
                    next_end = (end_i + 1) % n

                    c_prev = tour[prev_i]
                    c_start = tour[i]
                    c_end = tour[end_i]
                    c_next = tour[next_end]

                    if seg_len == 1 and (c_prev == c_next):
                        continue

                    # cost of removing segment
                    old_cost = dist(c_prev, c_start) + dist(c_end, c_next)
                    new_link = dist(c_prev, c_next)
                    removal_gain = old_cost - new_link

                    # try inserting after each neighbor of c_start
                    for cj in neighbors[c_start]:
                        j = pos[cj]
                        j1 = (j + 1) % n
                        cj1 = tour[j1]
                        # skip if j is within the segment
                        skip = False
                        for k2 in range(seg_len):
                            if tour[(i + k2) % n] == cj or tour[(i + k2) % n] == cj1:
                                skip = True
                                break
                        if skip:
                            continue
                        if j == prev_i or j1 == i:
                            continue

                        insert_cost = dist(cj, c_start) + dist(c_end, cj1) - dist(cj, cj1)
                        gain = removal_gain - insert_cost
                        if gain > 0:
                            # rebuild tour
                            seg = [tour[(i + k2) % n] for k2 in range(seg_len)]
                            # remove segment from tour
                            new_tour = []
                            idx2 = next_end
                            for _ in range(n - seg_len):
                                new_tour.append(tour[idx2])
                                idx2 = (idx2 + 1) % n
                            # find position of cj in new_tour
                            pos2 = {c2: k2 for k2, c2 in enumerate(new_tour)}
                            ins = pos2[cj]
                            new_tour = new_tour[:ins+1] + seg + new_tour[ins+1:]
                            tour = new_tour
                            for k2, c2 in enumerate(tour):
                                pos[c2] = k2
                            improved = True
                            break
        return tour

    # Double-bridge perturbation
    def double_bridge(tour):
        pos_list = sorted(random.sample(range(1, n), 4))
        a, b, c, d = pos_list
        new_tour = tour[:a] + tour[c:d] + tour[b:c] + tour[a:b] + tour[d:]
        return new_tour

    # Build initial tour
    best_tour = nn_tour(0)
    best_tour = two_opt(best_tour)
    best_tour = or_opt(best_tour)
    best_len = tour_length(best_tour)

    current_tour = best_tour[:]

    # ILS: iterated local search with double-bridge
    while time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.90:
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.85:
            break
        perturbed = double_bridge(current_tour)
        perturbed = two_opt(perturbed)
        if time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.80:
            perturbed = or_opt(perturbed)
        length = tour_length(perturbed)
        if length < best_len:
            best_len = length
            best_tour = perturbed[:]
            current_tour = perturbed[:]
        else:
            # Accept with some probability or reset to best
            current_tour = best_tour[:]

    return best_tour

import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    n = len(cities)
    budget = 0.04 * n

    def dist(i, j):
        dx = cities[i][0] - cities[j][0]
        dy = cities[i][1] - cities[j][1]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # Build candidate lists (k nearest neighbors)
    k = min(10, n - 1)
    cands = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = [(dist(i, j), j) for j in range(n) if j != i]
        dists.sort()
        cands.append([j for _, j in dists[:k]])

    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(dist(tour[i], tour[(i+1) % n]) for i in range(n))

    # Nearest neighbor construction
    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False] * n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            cur = tour[-1]
            best_d = float('inf')
            best_j = -1
            for j in cands[cur]:
                if not visited[j]:
                    d = dist(cur, j)
                    if d < best_d:
                        best_d = d
                        best_j = j
            if best_j == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        d = dist(cur, j)
                        if d < best_d:
                            best_d = d
                            best_j = j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = True
        return tour

    # Build position array
    def make_pos(tour):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        return pos

    # 2-opt with candidate lists and don't-look bits
    def two_opt(tour):
        pos = make_pos(tour)
        improved = True
        dnb = [False] * n
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    return tour
                if dnb[idx]:
                    continue
                i = idx
                ci = tour[i]
                ci_next = tour[(i + 1) % n]
                d_ci = dist(ci, ci_next)
                found = False
                for cj in cands[ci]:
                    j = pos[cj]
                    if dist(ci, cj) >= d_ci:
                        break
                    if j == i or j == (i + 1) % n:
                        continue
                    j_next = (j + 1) % n
                    cj_next = tour[j_next]
                    gain = d_ci + dist(cj, cj_next) - dist(ci, cj) - dist(ci_next, cj_next)
                    if gain > 0:
                        # Reverse segment between i+1 and j
                        a, b = (i + 1) % n, j
                        if a <= b:
                            tour[a:b+1] = tour[a:b+1][::-1]
                        else:
                            seg = tour[a:] + tour[:b+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            tour[a:] = seg[:n-a]
                            tour[:b+1] = seg[n-a:]
                        pos = make_pos(tour)
                        improved = True
                        found = True
                        dnb[ci] = False
                        dnb[ci_next] = False
                        dnb[cj] = False
                        dnb[cj_next] = False
                        break
                if not found:
                    dnb[idx] = True
        return tour

    # Or-opt: move segments of length 1, 2, 3
    def or_opt(tour):
        pos = make_pos(tour)
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for seg_len in [1, 2, 3]:
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    return tour
                for i in range(n):
                    if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                        return tour
                    # segment is tour[i..i+seg_len-1]
                    i_prev = (i - 1) % n
                    i_last = (i + seg_len - 1) % n
                    i_next = (i + seg_len) % n
                    
                    c_prev = tour[i_prev]
                    c_first = tour[i]
                    c_last = tour[i_last]
                    c_next = tour[i_next]
                    
                    # Cost of removing segment
                    remove_gain = dist(c_prev, c_first) + dist(c_last, c_next) - dist(c_prev, c_next)
                    
                    # Try inserting after each candidate of c_first
                    for cj in cands[c_first]:
                        j = pos[cj]
                        # Check j is not in the segment
                        in_seg = False
                        tmp = i
                        for _ in range(seg_len):
                            if tmp == j:
                                in_seg = True
                                break
                            tmp = (tmp + 1) % n
                        if in_seg or j == i_prev:
                            continue
                        
                        j_next = (j + 1) % n
                        c_j = tour[j]
                        c_j_next = tour[j_next]
                        
                        insert_cost = dist(c_j, c_first) + dist(c_last, c_j_next) - dist(c_j, c_j_next)
                        
                        if remove_gain - insert_cost > 0:
                            # Perform the move: rebuild tour
                            seg = []
                            tmp = i
                            for _ in range(seg_len):
                                seg.append(tour[tmp])
                                tmp = (tmp + 1) % n
                            
                            new_tour = []
                            tmp = i_next
                            while tmp != i:
                                new_tour.append(tour[tmp])
                                tmp = (tmp + 1) % n
                            
                            # Find insertion point in new_tour
                            ins = new_tour.index(c_j)
                            new_tour = new_tour[:ins+1] + seg + new_tour[ins+1:]
                            
                            tour = new_tour
                            pos = make_pos(tour)
                            improved = True
                            break
        return tour

    # Double-bridge perturbation
    def double_bridge(tour):
        # Pick 4 random cut points
        positions = sorted(random.sample(range(n), 4))
        a, b, c, d = positions
        # Reconnect in double-bridge fashion
        new_tour = (tour[:a+1] + tour[c+1:d+1] + 
                    tour[b+1:c+1] + tour[a+1:b+1] + tour[d+1:])
        return new_tour

    # Local search combining 2-opt and or-opt
    def local_search(tour):
        tour = two_opt(tour)
        if time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.95:
            tour = or_opt(tour)
        if time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.95:
            tour = two_opt(tour)
        return tour

    # Initial solution
    best_tour = nn_tour(0)
    best_tour = local_search(best_tour)
    best_len = tour_length(best_tour)

    # ILS loop
    while time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.90:
        # Perturb
        new_tour = double_bridge(best_tour[:])
        # Local search on perturbed
        new_tour = local_search(new_tour)
        new_len = tour_length(new_tour)
        if new_len < best_len:
            best_len = new_len
            best_tour = new_tour

    return best_tour

payload.code:
import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    n = len(cities)
    budget = 0.04 * n

    # TSPLIB nint distance
    def dist(i, j):
        dx = cities[i][0] - cities[j][0]
        dy = cities[i][1] - cities[j][1]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # Precompute k-nearest neighbors for candidate lists
    K = min(15, n - 1)
    # Build neighbor lists
    neighbors = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = []
        for j in range(n):
            if j != i:
                dx = cities[i][0] - cities[j][0]
                dy = cities[i][1] - cities[j][1]
                dists.append((dx*dx + dy*dy, j))
        dists.sort()
        neighbors.append([j for _, j in dists[:K]])

    # Precompute distance matrix for small n, else use function
    if n <= 400:
        D = [[0]*n for _ in range(n)]
        for i in range(n):
            xi, yi = cities[i]
            for j in range(i+1, n):
                dx = xi - cities[j][0]
                dy = yi - cities[j][1]
                d = int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)
                D[i][j] = d
                D[j][i] = d
        def d(i, j): return D[i][j]
    else:
        def d(i, j): return dist(i, j)

    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(d(tour[i], tour[(i+1) % n]) for i in range(n))

    # Nearest neighbor construction from start city
    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False] * n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            cur = tour[-1]
            best_d = float('inf')
            best_j = -1
            # Check candidates first
            for j in neighbors[cur]:
                if not visited[j]:
                    dd = d(cur, j)
                    if dd < best_d:
                        best_d = dd
                        best_j = j
            if best_j == -1:
                # fallback: check all
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        dd = d(cur, j)
                        if dd < best_d:
                            best_d = dd
                            best_j = j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = True
        return tour

    # 2-opt with candidate lists and don't-look bits
    def two_opt(tour):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        improved = True
        dont_look = [False] * n
        while improved:
            improved = False
            if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                break
            for idx in range(n):
                if dont_look[idx]:
                    continue
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    break
                i = idx
                city_i = tour[i]
                city_i1 = tour[(i + 1) % n]
                d_i = d(city_i, city_i1)
                found = False
                for city_j in neighbors[city_i]:
                    j = pos[city_j]
                    # Check gain potential
                    if d(city_i, city_j) >= d_i:
                        continue
                    j1 = (j + 1) % n
                    city_j1 = tour[j1]
                    if city_j == city_i1 or city_j1 == city_i:
                        continue
                    gain = d_i + d(city_j, city_j1) - d(city_i, city_j) - d(city_i1, city_j1)
                    if gain > 0:
                        # Reverse segment between i+1 and j
                        i1 = (i + 1) % n
                        if i1 <= j:
                            tour[i1:j+1] = tour[i1:j+1][::-1]
                        else:
                            # wrap-around: more complex
                            seg = tour[i1:] + tour[:j+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            l1 = n - i1
                            tour[i1:] = seg[:l1]
                            tour[:j+1] = seg[l1:]
                        # Update pos
                        for k in range(n):
                            pos[tour[k]] = k
                        improved = True
                        found = True
                        dont_look[city_i] = False
                        dont_look[city_i1] = False
                        dont_look[city_j] = False
                        dont_look[city_j1] = False
                        break
                if not found:
                    dont_look[idx] = True
        return tour

    # Or-opt: move segments of size 1, 2, 3
    def or_opt(tour):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                break
            for seg_len in [1, 2, 3]:
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    break
                for i in range(n):
                    if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                        break
                    # Segment: tour[i], tour[i+1], ..., tour[i+seg_len-1]
                    prev_i = (i - 1) % n
                    end_i = (i + seg_len - 1) % n
                    next_end = (end_i + 1) % n

                    c_prev = tour[prev_i]
                    c_start = tour[i]
                    c_end = tour[end_i]
                    c_next = tour[next_end]

                    if seg_len == 1 and (c_prev == c_next):
                        continue

                    # Cost of removing segment
                    remove_gain = d(c_prev, c_start) + d(c_end, c_next) - d(c_prev, c_next)

                    # Try inserting after each neighbor of c_start
                    for nb in neighbors[c_start]:
                        j = pos[nb]
                        # Skip positions within or adjacent to segment
                        # Check if j is in the segment
                        in_seg = False
                        for s in range(seg_len):
                            if j == (i + s) % n:
                                in_seg = True
                                break
                        if in_seg or j == prev_i:
                            continue

                        j_next = (j + 1) % n
                        c_j = tour[j]
                        c_j_next = tour[j_next]

                        # Skip if j_next is in segment
                        in_seg2 = False
                        for s in range(seg_len):
                            if j_next == (i + s) % n:
                                in_seg2 = True
                                break
                        if in_seg2:
                            continue

                        insert_cost = d(c_j, c_start) + d(c_end, c_j_next) - d(c_j, c_j_next)
                        gain = remove_gain - insert_cost

                        if gain > 0:
                            # Perform the move
                            segment = [tour[(i + s) % n] for s in range(seg_len)]
                            # Build new tour
                            new_tour = []
                            idx2 = next_end
                            while idx2 != j_next:
                                new_tour.append(tour[idx2])
                                idx2 = (idx2 + 1) % n
                            new_tour.append(tour[j_next] if False else None)
                            # Rebuild properly
                            # Remove segment from tour, insert after j
                            t2 = []
                            k = next_end
                            count = 0
                            while count < n - seg_len:
                                t2.append(tour[k])
                                k = (k + 1) % n
                                count += 1
                            # Find position of c_j in t2
                            ins_pos = t2.index(c_j)
                            for s, city in enumerate(segment):
                                t2.insert(ins_pos + 1 + s, city)
                            tour = t2
                            for k2 in range(n):
                                pos[tour[k2]] = k2
                            improved = True
                            break
                    if improved:
                        break
                if improved:
                    break
        return tour

    best_tour = None
    best_len = float('inf')

    # Run multiple starts
    starts = list(range(n))
    random.shuffle(starts)

    start_idx = 0
    while time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.9:
        if start_idx >= len(starts):
            # Restart from random permutation
            start = random.randint(0, n - 1)
        else:
            start = starts[start_idx]
            start_idx += 1

        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.85:
            break

        tour = nn_tour(start)

        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.85:
            tl = tour_length(tour)
            if tl < best_len:
                best_len = tl
                best_tour = tour[:]
            break

        tour = two_opt(tour)

        if time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.85:
            tour = or_opt(tour)

        tl = tour_length(tour)
        if tl < best_len:
            best_len = tl
            best_tour = tour[:]

    if best_tour is None:
        best_tour = list(range(n))

    return best_tour

You work on branch "ILS_vectorized_numpy".
HYPOTHESIS: Combining NumPy-vectorized distance matrix precomputation and candidate-list acceleration (from nearest_neighbor_multistart_2opt_numpy) with the double-bridge perturbation + aggressive local search ILS framework (from ILS_doubleBridge_LK) will reduce mean gap below 0.8%. The vectorized distance lookups eliminate the speed bottleneck that limits ILS iteration count, while double-bridge perturbation escapes 2-opt/3-opt local optima that multi-start alone cannot. Together they address both initialization diversity and local search depth within the 9-second budget.
STRATEGY: NumPy-vectorized distance matrix + candidate neighbor lists feeding an ILS loop with greedy NN initialization, 3-opt local search with don't-look bits, and double-bridge 4-opt perturbation using accept-if-improved elitism.
ROUND: 2. TIME LIMIT: your solve() must finish well under 10s.

Write pure-Python (stdlib only) defining exactly one entry point:
    def solve(cities: list[list[float]]) -> list[int]
`cities` is a list of [x, y] coordinates. Return a tour: a permutation of all city indices 0..N-1 (closed tour implied). Your solve() is executed SEPARATELY on each benchmark instance (100-575 cities each — large enough that reaching the optimum in the time budget is hard). The code must be COMPLETE and self-contained (define every helper; no placeholders) and TIME-BOUNDED: capture t0=time.time() and keep improvement loops inside `while time.time()-t0 < budget`, returning the best tour found so far before the per-instance limit — a solver that times out on any instance scores nothing for the whole run. Edge lengths are rounded to the nearest integer (TSPLIB EUC_2D), and your tour competes against the known optimum. The baseline is already nearest-neighbor + 2-opt, so go beyond plain 2-opt (Or-opt moves, candidate lists, don't-look bits, perturbation/restarts within the time budget). For reference, the state-of-the-art solver (LKH) reaches ~0% gap on these; aim as close to that as the time budget allows. Generalize: the final solver is re-tested on hidden instances. No imports beyond the standard library; no I/O; no threads.
INSTANCE: TSPLIB95 dev set: kroA100 (100 cities), kroA200 (200 cities), a280 (280 cities), lin318 (318 cities). Optima are known; score is the mean gap %% above optimum across all dev instances (TSPLIB rounded-integer metric). 4 held-out instances are kept hidden for final verification.

YOUR CURRENT BEST CODE (score None):
```python
import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    n = len(cities)
    budget = 0.04 * n

    def dist(i, j):
        dx = cities[i][0] - cities[j][0]
        dy = cities[i][1] - cities[j][1]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # Build candidate lists (k nearest neighbors)
    k = min(10, n - 1)
    cands = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = [(dist(i, j), j) for j in range(n) if j != i]
        dists.sort()
        cands.append([j for _, j in dists[:k]])

    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(dist(tour[i], tour[(i+1) % n]) for i in range(n))

    # Nearest neighbor construction
    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False] * n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            cur = tour[-1]
            best_d = float('inf')
            best_j = -1
            for j in cands[cur]:
                if not visited[j]:
                    d = dist(cur, j)
                    if d < best_d:
                        best_d = d
                        best_j = j
            if best_j == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        d = dist(cur, j)
                        if d < best_d:
                            best_d = d
                            best_j = j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = True
        return tour

    # Build position array
    def make_pos(tour):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        return pos

    # 2-opt with candidate lists and don't-look bits
    def two_opt(tour):
        pos = make_pos(tour)
        improved = True
        dnb = [False] * n
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    return tour
                if dnb[idx]:
                    continue
                i = idx
                ci = tour[i]
                ci_next = tour[(i + 1) % n]
                d_ci = dist(ci, ci_next)
                found = False
                for cj in cands[ci]:
                    j = pos[cj]
                    if dist(ci, cj) >= d_ci:
                        break
                    if j == i or j == (i + 1) % n:
                        continue
                    j_next = (j + 1) % n
                    cj_next = tour[j_next]
                    gain = d_ci + dist(cj, cj_next) - dist(ci, cj) - dist(ci_next, cj_next)
                    if gain > 0:
                        # Reverse segment between i+1 and j
                        a, b = (i + 1) % n, j
                        if a <= b:
                            tour[a:b+1] = tour[a:b+1][::-1]
                        else:
                            seg = tour[a:] + tour[:b+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            tour[a:] = seg[:n-a]
                            tour[:b+1] = seg[n-a:]
                        pos = make_pos(tour)
                        improved = True
                        found = True
                        dnb[ci] = False
                        dnb[ci_next] = False
                        dnb[cj] = False
                        dnb[cj_next] = False
                        break
                if not found:
                    dnb[idx] = True
        return tour

    # Or-opt: move segments of length 1, 2, 3
    def or_opt(tour):
        pos = make_pos(tour)
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for seg_len in [1, 2, 3]:
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    return tour
                for i in range(n):
                    if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                        return tour
                    # segment is tour[i..i+seg_len-1]
                    i_prev = (i - 1) % n
                    i_last = (i + seg_len - 1) % n
                    i_next = (i + seg_len) % n
                    
                    c_prev = tour[i_prev]
                    c_first = tour[i]
                    c_last = tour[i_last]
                    c_next = tour[i_next]
                    
                    # Cost of removing segment
                    remove_gain = dist(c_prev, c_first) + dist(c_last, c_next) - dist(c_prev, c_next)
                    
                    # Try inserting after each candidate of c_first
                    for cj in cands[c_first]:
                        j = pos[cj]
                        # Check j is not in the segment
                        in_seg = False
                        tmp = i
                        for _ in range(seg_len):
                            if tmp == j:
                                in_seg = True
                                break
                            tmp = (tmp + 1) % n
                        if in_seg or j == i_prev:
                            continue
                        
                        j_next = (j + 1) % n
                        c_j = tour[j]
                        c_j_next = tour[j_next]
                        
                        insert_cost = dist(c_j, c_first) + dist(c_last, c_j_next) - dist(c_j, c_j_next)
                        
                        if remove_gain - insert_cost > 0:
                            # Perform the move: rebuild tour
                            seg = []
                            tmp = i
                            for _ in range(seg_len):
                                seg.append(tour[tmp])
                                tmp = (tmp + 1) % n
                            
                            new_tour = []
                            tmp = i_next
                            while tmp != i:
                                new_tour.append(tour[tmp])
                                tmp = (tmp + 1) % n
                            
                            # Find insertion point in new_tour
                            ins = new_tour.index(c_j)
                            new_tour = new_tour[:ins+1] + seg + new_tour[ins+1:]
                            
                            tour = new_tour
                            pos = make_pos(tour)
                            improved = True
                            break
        return tour

    # Double-bridge perturbation
    def double_bridge(tour):
        # Pick 4 random cut points
        positions = sorted(random.sample(range(n), 4))
        a, b, c, d = positions
        # Reconnect in double-bridge fashion
        new_tour = (tour[:a+1] + tour[c+1:d+1] + 
                    tour[b+1:c+1] + tour[a+1:b+1] + tour[d+1:])
        return new_tour

    # Local search combining 2-opt and or-opt
    def local_search(tour):
        tour = two_opt(tour)
        if time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.95:
            tour = or_opt(tour)
        if time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.95:
            tour = two_opt(tour)
        return tour

    # Initial solution
    best_tour = nn_tour(0)
    best_tour = local_search(best_tour)
    best_len = tour_length(best_tour)

    # ILS loop
    while time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.90:
        # Perturb
        new_tour = double_bridge(best_tour[:])
        # Local search on perturbed
        new_tour = local_search(new_tour)
        new_len = tour_length(new_tour)
        if new_len < best_len:
            best_len = new_len
            best_tour = new_tour

    return best_tour
```

import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    n = len(cities)
    budget = 0.04 * n

    # Precompute distance matrix as flat list for O(1) access
    xs = [c[0] for c in cities]
    ys = [c[1] for c in cities]
    
    # Build distance matrix
    D = [0] * (n * n)
    for i in range(n):
        xi, yi = xs[i], ys[i]
        for j in range(n):
            if i != j:
                dx = xi - xs[j]
                dy = yi - ys[j]
                D[i * n + j] = int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    def d(i, j):
        return D[i * n + j]

    # Build candidate lists (k nearest neighbors)
    k = min(20, n - 1)
    cands = []
    for i in range(n):
        row = [(D[i * n + j], j) for j in range(n) if j != i]
        row.sort()
        cands.append([j for _, j in row[:k]])

    def tour_length(tour):
        s = 0
        for i in range(n):
            s += D[tour[i] * n + tour[(i + 1) % n]]
        return s

    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = bytearray(n)
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = 1
        cur = start
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            best_d = 10**18
            best_j = -1
            for j in cands[cur]:
                if not visited[j]:
                    dd = D[cur * n + j]
                    if dd < best_d:
                        best_d = dd
                        best_j = j
            if best_j == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        dd = D[cur * n + j]
                        if dd < best_d:
                            best_d = dd
                            best_j = j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = 1
            cur = best_j
        return tour

    def make_pos(tour):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i in range(n):
            pos[tour[i]] = i
        return pos

    def two_opt(tour):
        pos = make_pos(tour)
        dnb = bytearray(n)
        improved = True
        while improved:
            if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.92:
                return tour
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.92:
                    return tour
                if dnb[idx]:
                    continue
                i = idx
                ci = tour[i]
                ni = (i + 1) % n
                ci_next = tour[ni]
                d_ci = D[ci * n + ci_next]
                found = False
                for cj in cands[ci]:
                    dij = D[ci * n + cj]
                    if dij >= d_ci:
                        break
                    j = pos[cj]
                    if j == i or j == ni:
                        continue
                    nj = (j + 1) % n
                    cj_next = tour[nj]
                    gain = d_ci + D[cj * n + cj_next] - dij - D[ci_next * n + cj_next]
                    if gain > 0:
                        # Reverse segment between ni and j
                        a, b = ni, j
                        if a <= b:
                            tour[a:b+1] = tour[a:b+1][::-1]
                        else:
                            seg = tour[a:] + tour[:b+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            tour[a:] = seg[:n - a]
                            tour[:b+1] = seg[n - a:]
                        pos = make_pos(tour)
                        improved = True
                        found = True
                        dnb[ci] = 0
                        dnb[ci_next] = 0
                        dnb[cj] = 0
                        dnb[cj_next] = 0
                        break
                if not found:
                    dnb[idx] = 1
        return tour

    def or_opt(tour):
        pos = make_pos(tour)
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for seg_len in [1, 2, 3]:
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.92:
                    return tour
                for i in range(n):
                    if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.92:
                        return tour
                    i_prev = (i - 1) % n
                    i_last = (i + seg_len - 1) % n
                    i_next = (i + seg_len) % n

                    c_prev = tour[i_prev]
                    c_first = tour[i]
                    c_last = tour[i_last]
                    c_next = tour[i_next]

                    remove_gain = (D[c_prev * n + c_first] + D[c_last * n + c_next]
                                   - D[c_prev * n + c_next])

                    for cj in cands[c_first]:
                        j = pos[cj]
                        # Check not in segment
                        in_seg = False
                        tmp = i
                        for _ in range(seg_len):
                            if tmp == j:
                                in_seg = True
                                break
                            tmp = (tmp + 1) % n
                        if in_seg or j == i_prev:
                            continue

                        j_next_idx = (j + 1) % n
                        c_j = tour[j]
                        c_j_next = tour[j_next_idx]

                        insert_cost = (D[c_j * n + c_first] + D[c_last * n + c_j_next]
                                       - D[c_j * n + c_j_next])

                        if remove_gain - insert_cost > 0:
                            # Extract segment
                            seg = []
                            tmp = i
                            for _ in range(seg_len):
                                seg.append(tour[tmp])
                                tmp = (tmp + 1) % n

                            # Build new tour without segment
                            new_tour = []
                            tmp = i_next
                            while tmp != i:
                                new_tour.append(tour[tmp])
                                tmp = (tmp + 1) % n

                            # Insert segment after c_j
                            ins = new_tour.index(c_j)
                            new_tour = new_tour[:ins+1] + seg + new_tour[ins+1:]

                            tour = new_tour
                            pos = make_pos(tour)
                            improved = True
                            break
        return tour

    def double_bridge(tour):
        positions = sorted(random.sample(range(1, n), 4))
        a, b, c, d = positions
        new_tour = (tour[:a] + tour[c:d] +
                    tour[b:c] + tour[a:b] + tour[d:])
        return new_tour

    def local_search(tour):
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.92:
            return tour
        tour = two_opt(tour)
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.92:
            return tour
        tour = or_opt(tour)
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.92:
            return tour
        tour = two_opt(tour)
        return tour

    # Multi-start initialization: try a few NN starts
    best_tour = nn_tour(0)
    best_tour = local_search(best_tour)
    best_len = tour_length(best_tour)

    # Try a couple more starts if time allows
    for start in range(1, min(n, 5)):
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.5:
            break
        t = nn_tour(start)
        t = local_search(t)
        tl = tour_length(t)
        if tl < best_len:
            best_len = tl
            best_tour = t

    # ILS loop with double-bridge
    while time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.92:
        new_tour = double_bridge(best_tour[:])
        new_tour = local_search(new_tour)
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.92:
            new_len = tour_length(new_tour)
            if new_len < best_len:
                best_len = new_len
                best_tour = new_tour
            break
        new_len = tour_length(new_tour)
        if new_len < best_len:
            best_len = new_len
            best_tour = new_tour

    return best_tour
```

import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    n = len(cities)
    budget = 0.04 * n

    def dist(i, j):
        dx = cities[i][0] - cities[j][0]
        dy = cities[i][1] - cities[j][1]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # Build candidate lists (k nearest neighbors)
    k = min(15, n - 1)
    neighbors = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = [(dist(i, j), j) for j in range(n) if j != i]
        dists.sort()
        neighbors.append([j for _, j in dists[:k]])

    # Greedy nearest-neighbor construction from best of several starts
    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False] * n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            cur = tour[-1]
            best_d = float('inf')
            best_j = -1
            for j in neighbors[cur]:
                if not visited[j]:
                    d = dist(cur, j)
                    if d < best_d:
                        best_d = d
                        best_j = j
            if best_j == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        d = dist(cur, j)
                        if d < best_d:
                            best_d = d
                            best_j = j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = True
        return tour

    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(dist(tour[i], tour[(i+1) % n]) for i in range(n))

    # Try a few starts
    best_tour = None
    best_len = float('inf')
    starts = random.sample(range(n), min(5, n))
    for s in starts:
        if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.3:
            break
        t = nn_tour(s)
        l = tour_length(t)
        if l < best_len:
            best_len = l
            best_tour = t[:]

    tour = best_tour[:]
    # Build position array
    pos = [0] * n
    for i, c in enumerate(tour):
        pos[c] = i

    def two_opt():
        improved = True
        dont_look = [False] * n
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if dont_look[idx]:
                    continue
                if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.85:
                    return
                i = tour[idx]
                found = False
                for j in neighbors[i]:
                    jdx = pos[j]
                    # Try edge (tour[idx], tour[(idx+1)%n]) vs (j, tour[(jdx+1)%n])
                    idx1 = (idx + 1) % n
                    jdx1 = (jdx + 1) % n
                    a, b = tour[idx], tour[idx1]
                    c, d = tour[jdx], tour[jdx1]
                    if b == c or a == d:
                        continue
                    d0 = dist(a, b) + dist(c, d)
                    d1 = dist(a, c) + dist(b, d)
                    if d1 < d0:
                        # Reverse segment between idx1 and jdx
                        if idx < jdx:
                            tour[idx1:jdx1] = tour[idx1:jdx1][::-1]
                        else:
                            tour[jdx1:idx1] = tour[jdx1:idx1][::-1]
                        for ii, cc in enumerate(tour):
                            pos[cc] = ii
                        improved = True
                        found = True
                        dont_look[idx] = False
                        break
                if not found:
                    dont_look[idx] = True

    two_opt()

    def or_opt_move(seg_len):
        """Try relocating segments of length seg_len."""
        improved = False
        for idx in range(n):
            if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.92:
                return False
            # Segment: tour[idx..idx+seg_len-1]
            seg = [tour[(idx + k) % n] for k in range(seg_len)]
            prev_idx = (idx - 1) % n
            next_idx = (idx + seg_len) % n
            prev_node = tour[prev_idx]
            next_node = tour[next_idx]
            seg_first = seg[0]
            seg_last = seg[-1]
            # Cost of removing segment
            remove_gain = dist(prev_node, seg_first) + dist(seg_last, next_node) - dist(prev_node, next_node)
            # Try inserting after each neighbor of seg_first
            for ins_node in neighbors[seg_first]:
                ins_idx = pos[ins_node]
                # Make sure ins_idx is not inside the segment
                in_seg = False
                for k in range(seg_len):
                    if (idx + k) % n == ins_idx:
                        in_seg = True
                        break
                if in_seg or ins_idx == prev_idx:
                    continue
                ins_next_idx = (ins_idx + 1) % n
                ins_next = tour[ins_next_idx]
                # Cost of inserting
                insert_cost = dist(ins_node, seg_first) + dist(seg_last, ins_next) - dist(ins_node, ins_next)
                if remove_gain > insert_cost:
                    # Perform move: remove segment, insert after ins_node
                    # Rebuild tour
                    flat = []
                    i2 = next_idx
                    while i2 != idx:
                        flat.append(tour[i2])
                        i2 = (i2 + 1) % n
                    # Find position of ins_node in flat
                    # Rebuild as list
                    new_tour = []
                    start_i = next_idx
                    cur = start_i
                    inserted = False
                    for _ in range(n - seg_len):
                        new_tour.append(tour[cur])
                        if tour[cur] == ins_node and not inserted:
                            new_tour.extend(seg)
                            inserted = True
                        cur = (cur + 1) % n
                    if not inserted:
                        new_tour.extend(seg)
                    if len(new_tour) == n:
                        tour[:] = new_tour
                        for ii, cc in enumerate(tour):
                            pos[cc] = ii
                        improved = True
                        break
        return improved

    # Or-opt passes
    for seg_len in [1, 2, 3]:
        if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.90:
            break
        iters = 0
        while time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.92 and iters < 3:
            if not or_opt_move(seg_len):
                break
            iters += 1

    # Final 2-opt pass
    two_opt()

    return tour
```

import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    n = len(cities)
    budget = 0.04 * n

    def dist(i, j):
        dx = cities[i][0] - cities[j][0]
        dy = cities[i][1] - cities[j][1]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # Precompute distance cache for small n
    D = [[0]*n for _ in range(n)]
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(i+1, n):
            d = dist(i, j)
            D[i][j] = d
            D[j][i] = d

    def d(i, j):
        return D[i][j]

    k = min(20, n - 1)
    neighbors = []
    for i in range(n):
        row = sorted(range(n), key=lambda j: D[i][j] if j != i else 10**9)
        neighbors.append(row[:k])

    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(D[tour[i]][tour[(i+1) % n]] for i in range(n))

    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False] * n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        cur = start
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            best_d = 10**9
            best_j = -1
            for j in neighbors[cur]:
                if not visited[j]:
                    dd = D[cur][j]
                    if dd < best_d:
                        best_d = dd
                        best_j = j
            if best_j == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        dd = D[cur][j]
                        if dd < best_d:
                            best_d = dd
                            best_j = j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = True
            cur = best_j
        return tour

    def two_opt(tour, pos, time_limit):
        improved = True
        dont_look = [False] * n
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if dont_look[idx]:
                    continue
                if time.time() - t0 > time_limit:
                    return
                i = tour[idx]
                idx1 = (idx + 1) % n
                b = tour[idx1]
                found = False
                d_ab = D[i][b]
                for j in neighbors[i]:
                    if D[i][j] >= d_ab:
                        break
                    jdx = pos[j]
                    jdx1 = (jdx + 1) % n
                    c = tour[jdx]
                    dd = tour[jdx1]
                    if dd == i or c == b:
                        continue
                    if d_ab + D[c][dd] > D[i][c] + D[b][dd]:
                        # Reverse between idx1 and jdx
                        if idx1 <= jdx:
                            tour[idx1:jdx+1] = tour[idx1:jdx+1][::-1]
                        else:
                            tour[jdx1:idx+1] = tour[jdx1:idx+1][::-1]
                        for ii in range(n):
                            pos[tour[ii]] = ii
                        improved = True
                        found = True
                        dont_look[idx] = False
                        dont_look[j] = False
                        break
                if not found:
                    dont_look[idx] = True

    def or_opt(tour, pos, time_limit):
        improved_any = False
        for seg_len in [1, 2, 3]:
            improved = True
            while improved and time.time() - t0 < time_limit:
                improved = False
                for idx in range(n):
                    if time.time() - t0 > time_limit:
                        return improved_any
                    prev_idx = (idx - 1) % n
                    next_idx = (idx + seg_len) % n
                    prev_node = tour[prev_idx]
                    seg_first = tour[idx]
                    seg_last = tour[(idx + seg_len - 1) % n]
                    next_node = tour[next_idx]
                    if prev_node == next_node:
                        continue
                    remove_gain = D[prev_node][seg_first] + D[seg_last][next_node] - D[prev_node][next_node]
                    best_gain = 0
                    best_ins = -1
                    best_rev = False
                    for ins_node in neighbors[seg_first]:
                        ins_idx = pos[ins_node]
                        # check not in segment
                        in_seg = False
                        for kk in range(seg_len):
                            if (idx + kk) % n == ins_idx:
                                in_seg = True
                                break
                        if in_seg or ins_idx == prev_idx:
                            continue
                        ins_next_idx = (ins_idx + 1) % n
                        ins_next = tour[ins_next_idx]
                        gain = remove_gain - (D[ins_node][seg_first] + D[seg_last][ins_next] - D[ins_node][ins_next])
                        if gain > best_gain:
                            best_gain = gain
                            best_ins = ins_node
                            best_rev = False
                        if seg_len > 1:
                            gain2 = remove_gain - (D[ins_node][seg_last] + D[seg_first][ins_next] - D[ins_node][ins_next])
                            if gain2 > best_gain:
                                best_gain = gain2
                                best_ins = ins_node
                                best_rev = True
                    if best_ins >= 0:
                        seg = [tour[(idx + kk) % n] for kk in range(seg_len)]
                        if best_rev:
                            seg = seg[::-1]
                        new_tour = []
                        cur = next_idx
                        inserted = False
                        for _ in range(n - seg_len):
                            new_tour.append(tour[cur])
                            if tour[cur] == best_ins and not inserted:
                                new_tour.extend(seg)
                                inserted = True
                            cur = (cur + 1) % n
                        if not inserted:
                            new_tour.extend(seg)
                        if len(new_tour) == n:
                            tour[:] = new_tour
                            for ii in range(n):
                                pos[tour[ii]] = ii
                            improved = True
                            improved_any = True
        return improved_any

    def double_bridge(tour):
        # 4-opt double bridge perturbation
        positions = sorted(random.sample(range(1, n), 4))
        a, b, c, d = positions
        new_tour = tour[:a] + tour[c:d] + tour[b:c] + tour[a:b] + tour[d:]
        return new_tour

    # Build initial solution
    best_tour = None
    best_len = float('inf')
    num_starts = min(5, n)
    starts = random.sample(range(n), num_starts)
    for s in starts:
        if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.25:
            break
        t = nn_tour(s)
        l = tour_length(t)
        if l < best_len:
            best_len = l
            best_tour = t[:]

    tour = best_tour[:]
    pos = [0] * n
    for i, c in enumerate(tour):
        pos[c] = i

    # Initial local search
    two_opt(tour, pos, budget * 0.5)
    or_opt(tour, pos, budget * 0.7)
    two_opt(tour, pos, budget * 0.8)

    best_tour = tour[:]
    best_len = tour_length(tour)

    # ILS with double-bridge
    while time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.95:
        if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.93:
            break
        # Perturb
        new_tour = double_bridge(best_tour)
        new_pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(new_tour):
            new_pos[c] = i

        remaining = budget * 0.95 - (time.time() - t0)
        if remaining < 0.01:
            break

        t_now = time.time() - t0
        two_opt(new_tour, new_pos, min(t_now + remaining * 0.5, budget * 0.93))
        or_opt(new_tour, new_pos, min(time.time() - t0 + remaining * 0.3, budget * 0.93))
        two_opt(new_tour, new_pos, min(time.time() - t0 + remaining * 0.2, budget * 0.93))

        new_len = tour_length(new_tour)
        if new_len < best_len:
            best_len = new_len
            best_tour = new_tour[:]

    return best_tour
```

import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    n = len(cities)
    budget = 0.04 * n

    # Precompute integer distances (TSPLIB EUC_2D)
    xs = [c[0] for c in cities]
    ys = [c[1] for c in cities]

    def dist(i, j):
        dx = xs[i] - xs[j]
        dy = ys[i] - ys[j]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # k-nearest neighbors
    K = min(15, n - 1)
    neighbors = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = [(dist(i, j), j) for j in range(n) if j != i]
        dists.sort()
        neighbors.append([j for _, j in dists[:K]])

    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(dist(tour[i], tour[(i+1) % n]) for i in range(n))

    # Nearest-neighbor construction
    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False] * n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            cur = tour[-1]
            best_d, best_j = float('inf'), -1
            for j in neighbors[cur]:
                if not visited[j]:
                    d = dist(cur, j)
                    if d < best_d:
                        best_d, best_j = d, j
                        break
            if best_j == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        d = dist(cur, j)
                        if d < best_d:
                            best_d, best_j = d, j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = True
        return tour

    # 2-opt with candidate lists and don't-look bits
    def two_opt(tour):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        improved = True
        dont_look = [False] * n
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    return tour
                if dont_look[tour[idx]]:
                    continue
                i = idx
                ci = tour[i]
                i1 = (i + 1) % n
                ci1 = tour[i1]
                d_ci = dist(ci, ci1)
                found = False
                for cj in neighbors[ci]:
                    j = pos[cj]
                    if dist(ci, cj) >= d_ci:
                        break
                    j1 = (j + 1) % n
                    cj1 = tour[j1]
                    if cj == ci or cj1 == ci:
                        continue
                    gain = d_ci + dist(cj, cj1) - dist(ci, cj) - dist(ci1, cj1)
                    if gain > 0:
                        # reverse segment between i1 and j
                        if i1 <= j:
                            tour[i1:j+1] = tour[i1:j+1][::-1]
                        else:
                            tour[j1:i+1] = tour[j1:i+1][::-1]
                        for k2, c2 in enumerate(tour):
                            pos[c2] = k2
                        improved = True
                        found = True
                        dont_look[ci] = False
                        break
                if not found:
                    dont_look[ci] = True
        return tour

    # Or-opt: relocate segments of length 1, 2, 3
    def or_opt(tour):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for seg_len in [1, 2, 3]:
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    return tour
                for i in range(n):
                    if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                        return tour
                    # segment: tour[i], tour[i+1], ..., tour[i+seg_len-1]
                    prev_i = (i - 1) % n
                    end_i = (i + seg_len - 1) % n
                    next_end = (end_i + 1) % n

                    c_prev = tour[prev_i]
                    c_start = tour[i]
                    c_end = tour[end_i]
                    c_next = tour[next_end]

                    if seg_len == 1 and (c_prev == c_next):
                        continue

                    # cost of removing segment
                    old_cost = dist(c_prev, c_start) + dist(c_end, c_next)
                    new_link = dist(c_prev, c_next)
                    removal_gain = old_cost - new_link

                    # try inserting after each neighbor of c_start
                    for cj in neighbors[c_start]:
                        j = pos[cj]
                        j1 = (j + 1) % n
                        cj1 = tour[j1]
                        # skip if j is within the segment
                        skip = False
                        for k2 in range(seg_len):
                            if tour[(i + k2) % n] == cj or tour[(i + k2) % n] == cj1:
                                skip = True
                                break
                        if skip:
                            continue
                        if j == prev_i or j1 == i:
                            continue

                        insert_cost = dist(cj, c_start) + dist(c_end, cj1) - dist(cj, cj1)
                        gain = removal_gain - insert_cost
                        if gain > 0:
                            # rebuild tour
                            seg = [tour[(i + k2) % n] for k2 in range(seg_len)]
                            # remove segment from tour
                            new_tour = []
                            idx2 = next_end
                            for _ in range(n - seg_len):
                                new_tour.append(tour[idx2])
                                idx2 = (idx2 + 1) % n
                            # find position of cj in new_tour
                            pos2 = {c2: k2 for k2, c2 in enumerate(new_tour)}
                            ins = pos2[cj]
                            new_tour = new_tour[:ins+1] + seg + new_tour[ins+1:]
                            tour = new_tour
                            for k2, c2 in enumerate(tour):
                                pos[c2] = k2
                            improved = True
                            break
        return tour

    # Double-bridge perturbation
    def double_bridge(tour):
        pos_list = sorted(random.sample(range(1, n), 4))
        a, b, c, d = pos_list
        new_tour = tour[:a] + tour[c:d] + tour[b:c] + tour[a:b] + tour[d:]
        return new_tour

    # Build initial tour
    best_tour = nn_tour(0)
    best_tour = two_opt(best_tour)
    best_tour = or_opt(best_tour)
    best_len = tour_length(best_tour)

    current_tour = best_tour[:]

    # ILS: iterated local search with double-bridge
    while time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.90:
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.85:
            break
        perturbed = double_bridge(current_tour)
        perturbed = two_opt(perturbed)
        if time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.80:
            perturbed = or_opt(perturbed)
        length = tour_length(perturbed)
        if length < best_len:
            best_len = length
            best_tour = perturbed[:]
            current_tour = perturbed[:]
        else:
            # Accept with some probability or reset to best
            current_tour = best_tour[:]

    return best_tour
```

```python
import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    n = len(cities)
    budget = 0.04 * n

    xs = [c[0] for c in cities]
    ys = [c[1] for c in cities]

    def dist(i, j):
        dx = xs[i] - xs[j]
        dy = ys[i] - ys[j]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # Precompute distance matrix for small n, else use function
    if n <= 300:
        D = [[0]*n for _ in range(n)]
        for i in range(n):
            for j in range(i+1, n):
                d = int(math.sqrt((xs[i]-xs[j])**2 + (ys[i]-ys[j])**2) + 0.5)
                D[i][j] = d
                D[j][i] = d
        def d(i, j): return D[i][j]
    else:
        def d(i, j):
            dx = xs[i] - xs[j]
            dy = ys[i] - ys[j]
            return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    K = min(20, n - 1)
    neighbors = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = sorted((d(i, j), j) for j in range(n) if j != i)
        neighbors.append([j for _, j in dists[:K]])

    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(d(tour[i], tour[(i+1) % n]) for i in range(n))

    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False] * n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            cur = tour[-1]
            best_d, best_j = float('inf'), -1
            for j in neighbors[cur]:
                if not visited[j]:
                    best_d, best_j = d(cur, j), j
                    break
            if best_j == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        dd = d(cur, j)
                        if dd < best_d:
                            best_d, best_j = dd, j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = True
        return tour

    def two_opt(tour, time_limit):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        dont_look = [False] * n
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= time_limit:
                    return tour
                ci = tour[idx]
                if dont_look[ci]:
                    continue
                i = idx
                i1 = (i + 1) % n
                ci1 = tour[i1]
                d_ci_ci1 = d(ci, ci1)
                found = False
                for cj in neighbors[ci]:
                    if d(ci, cj) >= d_ci_ci1:
                        break
                    j = pos[cj]
                    j1 = (j + 1) % n
                    cj1 = tour[j1]
                    if cj == ci1 or cj1 == ci:
                        continue
                    gain = d_ci_ci1 + d(cj, cj1) - d(ci, cj) - d(ci1, cj1)
                    if gain > 0:
                        # reverse segment i1..j or j1..i
                        if i < j:
                            l, r = i1, j
                        else:
                            l, r = j1, i
                        tour[l:r+1] = tour[l:r+1][::-1]
                        for k2 in range(l, r+1):
                            pos[tour[k2]] = k2
                        improved = True
                        found = True
                        dont_look[ci] = False
                        dont_look[ci1] = False
                        dont_look[cj] = False
                        dont_look[cj1] = False
                        break
                if not found:
                    dont_look[ci] = True
        return tour

    def or_opt(tour, time_limit):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for seg_len in [1, 2, 3]:
                if time.time() - t0 >= time_limit:
                    return tour
                dont_look = [False] * n
                for idx in range(n):
                    if time.time() - t0 >= time_limit:
                        return tour
                    ci = tour[idx]
                    if dont_look[ci]:
                        continue
                    i = idx
                    prev_i = (i - 1) % n
                    end_i = (i + seg_len - 1) % n
                    next_end = (end_i + 1) % n

                    c_prev = tour[prev_i]
                    c_start = tour[i]
                    c_end = tour[end_i]
                    c_next = tour[next_end]

                    if c_prev == c_next:
                        dont_look[ci] = True
                        continue

                    old_cost = d(c_prev, c_start) + d(c_end, c_next)
                    new_link = d(c_prev, c_next)
                    removal_gain = old_cost - new_link

                    found = False
                    for cj in neighbors[c_start]:
                        if d(c_start, cj) >= old_cost:
                            break
                        j = pos[cj]
                        j1 = (j + 1) % n
                        cj1 = tour[j1]
                        # check cj not in segment
                        in_seg = False
                        for k2 in range(seg_len):
                            if tour[(i + k2) % n] == cj:
                                in_seg = True
                                break
                        if in_seg:
                            continue
                        if j == prev_i or j1 == i:
                            continue
                        insert_cost = d(cj, c_start) + d(c_end, cj1) - d(cj, cj1)
                        if removal_gain - insert_cost > 0:
                            seg = [tour[(i + k2) % n] for k2 in range(seg_len)]
                            new_tour = []
                            idx2 = next_end
                            for _ in range(n - seg_len):
                                new_tour.append(tour[idx2])
                                idx2 = (idx2 + 1) % n
                            pos2 = {c2: k2 for k2, c2 in enumerate(new_tour)}
                            ins = pos2[cj]
                            tour = new_tour[:ins+1] + seg + new_tour[ins+1:]
                            for k2, c2 in enumerate(tour):
                                pos[c2] = k2
                            improved = True
                            found = True
                            break
                    if not found:
                        dont_look[ci] = True
        return tour

    def double_bridge(tour):
        positions = sorted(random.sample(range(1, n), 4))
        a, b, c, dd = positions
        return tour[:a] + tour[c:dd] + tour[b:c] + tour[a:b] + tour[dd:]

    def local_search(tour, time_limit):
        tour = two_opt(tour, time_limit)
        if time.time() - t0 < time_limit:
            tour = or_opt(tour, time_limit)
        return tour

    # Multi-start: try several NN starts
    best_tour = None
    best_len = float('inf')

    # Initial construction phase: use ~15% of budget
    init_budget = t0 + budget * 0.20
    starts = list(range(n))
    random.shuffle(starts)

    for start in starts:
        if time.time() - t0 >= init_budget:
            break
        tour = nn_tour(start)
        tl = tour_length(tour)
        if tl < best_len:
            best_len = tl
            best_tour = tour[:]

    # Initial local search
    ls_budget = t0 + budget * 0.50
    best_tour = local_search(best_tour, min(ls_budget, t0 + budget * 0.95) - t0 + t0)
    best_len = tour_length(best_tour)
    current_tour = best_tour[:]

    # ILS phase
    ils_budget = t0 + budget * 0.90
    while time.time() - t0 < ils_budget:
        remaining = ils_budget - time.time()
        if remaining < budget * 0.05:
            break
        perturbed = double_bridge(current_tour)
        iter_limit = min(time.time() + remaining * 0.7, ils_budget)
        perturbed = local_search(perturbed, iter_limit - t0 + t0)
        p_len = tour_length(perturbed)
        if p_len < best_len:
            best_len = p_len
            best_tour = perturbed[:]
            current_tour = perturbed[:]
        else:
            current_tour = best_tour[:]

    return best_tour

import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    n = len(cities)
    budget = 0.04 * n

    def dist(i, j):
        dx = cities[i][0] - cities[j][0]
        dy = cities[i][1] - cities[j][1]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # Build k-nearest neighbor lists
    K = min(15, n - 1)
    neighbors = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = []
        for j in range(n):
            if i != j:
                dists.append((dist(i, j), j))
        dists.sort()
        neighbors.append([j for _, j in dists[:K]])

    # Nearest neighbor construction from best of several starts
    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False] * n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            cur = tour[-1]
            best_d = float('inf')
            best_j = -1
            for j in neighbors[cur]:
                if not visited[j]:
                    d = dist(cur, j)
                    if d < best_d:
                        best_d = d
                        best_j = j
            if best_j == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        d = dist(cur, j)
                        if d < best_d:
                            best_d = d
                            best_j = j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = True
        return tour

    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(dist(tour[i], tour[(i+1) % n]) for i in range(n))

    # Try a few starts, pick best
    best_tour = None
    best_len = float('inf')
    starts = [0] + random.sample(range(n), min(4, n-1))
    for s in starts:
        if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.3:
            break
        t = nn_tour(s)
        l = tour_length(t)
        if l < best_len:
            best_len = l
            best_tour = t[:]

    tour = best_tour[:]

    # Position array: pos[city] = index in tour
    pos = [0] * n
    for i, c in enumerate(tour):
        pos[c] = i

    def two_opt():
        nonlocal tour, best_len
        improved = True
        dont_look = [False] * n
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.85:
                    return
                i = idx
                if dont_look[tour[i]]:
                    continue
                ci = tour[i]
                ci1 = tour[(i+1) % n]
                d1 = dist(ci, ci1)
                found = False
                for cj in neighbors[ci]:
                    j = pos[cj]
                    if j == i or j == (i+1) % n:
                        continue
                    cj1 = tour[(j+1) % n]
                    if cj == ci1:
                        continue
                    new_d = dist(ci, cj) + dist(ci1, cj1)
                    old_d = d1 + dist(cj, cj1)
                    if new_d < old_d - 1e-10:
                        # Reverse segment between i+1 and j
                        a, b = (i+1) % n, j
                        if a <= b:
                            seg = tour[a:b+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            tour[a:b+1] = seg
                        else:
                            # wrap-around: reverse a..n-1 + 0..b
                            seg = tour[a:] + tour[:b+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            tour[a:] = seg[:n-a]
                            tour[:b+1] = seg[n-a:]
                        for k2 in range(n):
                            pos[tour[k2]] = k2
                        best_len = tour_length(tour)
                        improved = True
                        found = True
                        dont_look[ci] = False
                        dont_look[cj] = False
                        break
                if not found:
                    dont_look[ci] = True

    two_opt()

    # Or-opt: move single cities and pairs
    def or_opt():
        nonlocal tour, best_len
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.92:
                return
            for seg_len in [1, 2, 3]:
                for i in range(n):
                    if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.92:
                        return
                    # segment: tour[i..i+seg_len-1]
                    i1 = (i + seg_len) % n
                    prev_i = (i - 1) % n
                    # Cost of removing segment
                    seg_start = tour[i]
                    seg_end = tour[(i + seg_len - 1) % n]
                    before = tour[prev_i]
                    after = tour[i1]
                    if before == seg_start or after == seg_end:
                        continue
                    remove_gain = dist(before, seg_start) + dist(seg_end, after) - dist(before, after)
                    if remove_gain <= 0:
                        continue
                    # Try inserting after neighbors of seg_start
                    for cj in neighbors[seg_start]:
                        j = pos[cj]
                        # Don't insert within the segment itself
                        in_seg = False
                        for k2 in range(seg_len):
                            if (i + k2) % n == j:
                                in_seg = True
                                break
                        if in_seg or j == prev_i:
                            continue
                        cj1 = tour[(j+1) % n]
                        insert_cost = dist(cj, seg_start) + dist(seg_end, cj1) - dist(cj, cj1)
                        if remove_gain - insert_cost > 1e-10:
                            # Perform the move
                            seg = [tour[(i+k2) % n] for k2 in range(seg_len)]
                            # Remove segment from tour
                            new_tour = []
                            skip = set((i+k2)%n for k2 in range(seg_len))
                            for k2 in range(n):
                                if k2 not in skip:
                                    new_tour.append(tour[k2])
                            # Find new position of j in new_tour
                            # Insert after cj
                            ins = new_tour.index(cj)
                            new_tour = new_tour[:ins+1] + seg + new_tour[ins+1:]
                            tour = new_tour
                            for k2 in range(n):
                                pos[tour[k2]] = k2
                            best_len = tour_length(tour)
                            improved = True
                            break

    or_opt()

    # Final 2-opt pass
    two_opt()

    return tour
```
